summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Severe right upper arm pain following a volleyball injury.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports right upper arm pain that began approximately 18 hours ago after spiking a volleyball. He describes falling while attempting the spike, landing directly on his arm, and experiencing immediate, severe pain. He has been taking ibuprofen every six hours, but reports minimal relief. He denies any prior injuries to the arm. He has gallstones and takes pepcid for it. He had a lumbar fusion six years ago.

Findings
Examination reveals swelling and erythema over the right shoulder, tenderness over the right shoulder, normal pulses, and no reported numbness or tingling in the right arm. X-ray demonstrates a proximal humerus fracture.

Assessment
Proximal humerus fracture.

Plan
Prescribe lortab 5mg every 6 hours as needed for pain, providing a prescription for 20 tablets. Order a long arm cast and sling. Schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess fracture alignment. Refer to physical therapy.
reference_summary:
CC:

Right upper arm pain.

HPI:

Ms. Russell is a 39-year-old female who presents today for an evaluation of severe right upper arm pain. She states she was playing volleyball yesterday and fell and landed on her arm. She has been taking ibuprofen every 6 hours but it does not help. She rates her pain 9/10. She denies any numbness or tingling in her arm. She has a history of gallstones and takes Pepcid. She has a past surgical history of a lumbar fusion 6 years ago. She denies any allergies.

EXAM

Examination of the right upper extremity shows swelling and erythema of the right shoulder. Tenderness over the right shoulder. Normal pulses.

RESULTS

X-rays of the right humerus, 2 views, obtained on today's visit show a proximal humerus fracture.

IMPRESSION

Right proximal humerus fracture.

PLAN

At this point, I discussed the diagnosis and treatment options with the patient. I have recommended we place her into a long arm cast and sling. She will follow up in 2 weeks for repeat imaging. At that point, we may need to discuss surigical options. I will prescribe Lortab, 5 mg, #20 (twenty) to take every 6 hours as needed for pain.

All questions were answered.

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Severe right upper arm pain following a volleyball injury.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports right upper arm pain that began approximately 18 hours ago after spiking a volleyball. He describes falling while attempting the spike, landing directly on his arm, and experiencing immediate, severe pain. He has been taking ibuprofen every six hours, but reports minimal relief. section_miscategorizations.text: He has gallstones and takes pepcid for it.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is past medical history and a chronic medication rather than directly related to the acute arm injury; in a more granular template it would belong in a Past Medical History/Medications section, but within this simplified template its placement in HPI is acceptable and not misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: He had a lumbar fusion six years ago.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is remote surgical history rather than part of the current illness; ideally it would be in a Past Surgical History section, but given the available sections it does not substantially distort the meaning of the HPI.
